description: Geopolitical and civilizational progression from 1917 to deep future. Models Turchin structural-demographic cycles, Khaldun asabiyyah decay, Tainter complexity collapse, Kondratiev waves, and Kardashev transitions. The external world the Soviet system exists within — trade, war, climate, technology, governance evolution. Grounded in academic research (Turchin, Tainter, Diamond, Khaldun) and hard SF projections (Baxter, Egan, Reynolds).

narrative.pravdaHeadline: IMPERIALIST AGGRESSION: Western Powers Form Anti-Soviet Alliance
narrative.toast: The wartime alliance crumbles. Churchill's 'Iron Curtain' speech signals decades of confrontation. Moscow increases vigilance quotas.
narrative.description: The Truman Doctrine (1947) formalizes US containment policy. NATO forms in 1949. The world divides into two armed camps. For the predsedatel, this means increased quotas, more KGB scrutiny, and military production targets.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA PROCLAIMED: World Revolution Advances
narrative.toast: Mao Zedong proclaims the People's Republic. 540 million people join the socialist camp. Moscow celebrates. Quietly, the competition for communist leadership begins.
narrative.description: October 1, 1949. The largest nation on Earth becomes communist. The Sino-Soviet alliance appears invincible. Within 10 years, ideological disagreements will fracture it completely.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: NUCLEAR BRINKMANSHIP: World Holds Breath as Superpowers Face Off Over Cuba
narrative.toast: For 13 days in October, humanity is 90 minutes from nuclear annihilation. Both sides step back. Détente becomes thinkable.
narrative.description: October 16-28, 1962. Soviet missiles in Cuba. American naval blockade. The closest the world came to nuclear war. Resolution leads to the Nuclear Test Ban Treaty (1963) and eventual détente.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: IDEOLOGICAL DISAGREEMENTS WITH CHINA: Soviet-Chinese Friendship Endures (Official Statement)
narrative.toast: The world's two largest communist nations have fundamental disagreements about revolutionary theory. Border incidents multiply. The friendship is formally unshaken. Nobody believes this.
narrative.description: By 1960, ideological differences between Moscow and Beijing become irreconcilable. Khrushchev's de-Stalinization horrifies Mao. Soviet advisors are withdrawn from China. By 1969, border clashes along the Ussuri River bring the two nuclear powers close to war.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: PEACEFUL COEXISTENCE ADVANCES: SALT Treaty Signed, Trade Expands
narrative.toast: The SALT arms limitation treaty is signed. Grain imports from America begin. Moscow's tension eases. The settlement's quota pressure does not.
narrative.description: Nixon visits Moscow (1972). SALT I limits strategic weapons. Helsinki Accords (1975) normalize European borders. Soviet grain imports from the US begin — the settlement may see foreign equipment for the first time.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: OPEC EMBARGO ENRICHES SOCIALIST CAMP: Soviet Oil Revenues Surge
narrative.toast: The 1973 OPEC oil embargo quadruples crude prices. Soviet hard currency earnings explode. Moscow uses petrodollars to fund everything from grain imports to space programs. This will not last.
narrative.description: The oil price shock of 1973 transforms the Soviet economy. Western Siberian oil fields become the USSR's ATM. Petrodollar wealth masks structural economic problems — stagnation, inefficiency, and declining innovation — that will prove fatal when oil prices crash in the 1980s.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: TEMPORARY MARKET FLUCTUATION: Oil Prices Adjust Downward
narrative.toast: Saudi Arabia floods the market. Oil drops from $30 to $10/barrel. Soviet hard currency revenue collapses by 50%. The word 'temporary' does a lot of work in the official statement.
narrative.description: The 1986 oil price collapse devastates the Soviet economy. The system built on petrodollars crumbles. Gorbachev's reforms become desperate necessity, not ideological choice. For the settlement, this means supply shortages, equipment breakdowns, and rising desperation.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: PARTY UNITY PREVAILS: August Coup Fails, Socialist Construction Continues
narrative.toast: The coup failed. Yeltsin is arrested. The Party holds. The USSR does not dissolve — it reforms, tightens, and prepares. History has taken the other path.
narrative.description: In this timeline, the August 1991 coup succeeded in some ways and failed in others — the reformers were contained, but the system that emerged is no longer the same system. A harder, leaner, angrier apparatus. The word 'Soviet' remains, but what it names has changed.

milestones.name: The Divorce of Ideology and Practice

narrative.pravdaHeadline: 5-YEAR PLAN TARGETS REVISED UPWARD: Collective Productivity Demonstrates Socialist Superiority
narrative.toast: The directive from Moscow is 47 pages. Page 14 contains the actual order. Pages 1-13 are Marxist preamble. No one mentions this.
narrative.scene:
The packet arrives on a Tuesday, as most of them do.

Comrade Chairman, by directive of the Central Planning Committee, your settlement is hereby required to increase grain deliveries by 18% in the current quarter, timber processing by 12%, and to submit a signed declaration affirming the continuing relevance of dialectical materialism to agricultural production. The declaration template is attached as Appendix C.

You read the appendix. It is six pages long. It contains phrases like 'the objective contradictions inherent in bourgeois soil management' and 'the teleological necessity of collective ownership as expressed through yield optimization.' You read it twice. You are still not certain what it is asking you to sign.

Your deputy has prepared the production numbers. The 18% increase is not possible this quarter. It may not be possible any quarter. The deputy knows this. You know this. Moscow almost certainly knows this. The declaration form, however, does not know this, and the form must be completed.

There is a correct way to handle this. You have seen the correct way. It involves numbers that are not numbers and signatures that mean something other than what signatures usually mean. Everyone does it. No one discusses it. The system requires it.

choices.label: Master the performance — file adjusted numbers
choices.description: Submit inflated figures with the ideological declaration. Everyone does this. It is the path of comfortable mediocrity.

onSuccess.announcement: The declaration is filed. The numbers are filed. Moscow acknowledges receipt with a form letter of commendation. Your deputy notes that the actual production was 6% below last quarter. You note that the commendation will look good in your personnel file. Both observations are correct.

onFailure.announcement: A statistical auditor arrives from the regional bureau three weeks later. She has cross-referenced your figures against rail transport manifests. There is a discrepancy. She files a report. You receive a black mark for 'administrative irregularities in planning documentation.' The irony is noted privately.

choices.label: Implement the directive literally — actual 18% increase
choices.description: Actually try to meet the quota. Workers on double shifts. Full Stakhanovite mobilization. This is not recommended.

onSuccess.announcement: Against every expectation, including your own, the settlement meets the target. Worker morale has collapsed to historic lows. Three workers have been hospitalized with exhaustion. Your deputy has resigned. Moscow sends a delegation to study your methods. You are not certain what they will conclude.

onFailure.announcement: The workers cannot sustain the pace. Production peaks in week two and collapses in week four. The final numbers are 3% below last quarter. You have filed a declaration affirming dialectical materialism. The contradiction is not resolved.

choices.label: File an accurate report noting the discrepancy
choices.description: Submit real numbers with a memo explaining the gap between directive and capacity. A small act of bureaucratic honesty with unpredictable consequences.

onSuccess.announcement: Your memo is read. Apparently someone in the statistical division has been waiting for exactly this documentation. A quiet reply arrives: the targets have been 'recalibrated' for your district. No explanation is given. You are not commended. You are not punished. The gap between what is said and what is done narrows, by exactly one memo, for exactly one quarter.

onFailure.announcement: Your memo is read by the wrong person. The regional inspector notes that you have submitted documentation suggesting 'insufficient ideological commitment to plan fulfillment.' A review is scheduled. You learn that accurate reporting is not, in this context, considered an administrative virtue.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: ETERNAL RUSSIA ENDURES: Motherland Spirit Guides Socialist Construction Into New Century
narrative.toast: The pamphlets stopped quoting Marx in 2031. By 2040 they quote Pushkin, folk songs, and a general feeling about soil. This is also called ideology.
narrative.description: The formal apparatus of Marxist-Leninist doctrine has been quietly replaced by something older and harder to argue with: the nation itself. The system still functions. The quotas still arrive. The forms still require signatures. But the language has changed. What is required now is not ideological purity but patriotic loyalty — a distinction that, in practice, changes very little, but changes everything.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: OCTOBER 2117: BICENTENNIAL CELEBRATIONS MARK 200 YEARS OF UNBROKEN SOCIALIST CONSTRUCTION
narrative.toast: The revolution is two hundred years ago. The workers celebrate it with a public holiday. Nobody alive remembers what it was about. This is fine. Empires run on myths, not memories.
narrative.description: By 2117, the October Revolution has become what all founding moments eventually become: a story told to justify the present. The details are contested, the principals are painted on murals in heroic poses, and the actual content of 1917 Bolshevism would be unrecognizable to the bureaucrats who invoke it. The apparatus persists not because of what it believes but because of what it is. Which is, historically, how things persist.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: NORTHERN INFRASTRUCTURE REQUIRES MAINTENANCE: Ministry of Permafrost Affairs Established
narrative.toast: 65% of Russia is built on frozen ground. It is thawing. 40% of northern infrastructure already damaged. $250 billion at risk. The Ministry of Permafrost Affairs has been created to manage the situation. They have requested a larger office.
narrative.description: Russia's permafrost underlies 65% of its territory and is warming at 2.5x the global average. Infrastructure damage already exceeds $100 billion. Pipeline ruptures, building foundation collapse, and road degradation accelerate. 45% of Arctic hydrocarbon extraction fields are in thaw-risk zones.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: FERTILIZER PRODUCTION TARGETS REVISED: Ministry of Agriculture Adapts to Resource Constraints
narrative.toast: Phosphate rock — essential for all modern agriculture — reaches peak extraction. 85% of remaining reserves are in Morocco. The Five-Year Agricultural Intensification Plan must now account for declining fertilizer availability.
narrative.description: Phosphorus has no substitute in agriculture. Global reserves peak around 2034. Agricultural yields decline without synthetic fertilizers. The Soviet system, which already struggles with agricultural efficiency, faces a structural constraint that no amount of quota-setting can overcome.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: ALGORITHMIC PLANNING COMMISSION ESTABLISHED: Machine Intelligence Serves the Plan
narrative.toast: The calculation problem that defeated Gosplan — coordinating 25 million products across 300 million people — is solved by artificial intelligence. Planning efficiency increases 30%. Corruption detection increases 50%. The bureaucrats are not pleased.
narrative.description: The Soviet calculation problem (Hayek's critique) is rendered moot by AI capable of processing millions of simultaneous resource allocation decisions. The system that couldn't track shoe sizes across 15 republics can now optimize global supply chains. But AI planning creates a new problem: what role for the Party if the machine does the planning? Cybernetics returns — Glushkov's dream realized 70 years late.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: POPULATION STRUCTURE ADJUSTMENTS: Labor Allocation Optimized for Demographic Transition
narrative.toast: Retirees outnumber workers. The pension system, designed for a growing population, cannot fund itself. Labor shortage in every sector. The Five-Year Plan has not accounted for the absence of young hands.
narrative.description: UN projections (2024 revision) show global population peaking around 2080. But the structural crisis hits earlier: dependency ratios invert by 2040 in developed nations. For the Soviet system, which relies on directed labor, the absence of young workers is existential.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: FUSION REACTOR ACHIEVES NET ENERGY: New Era of Power Production Begins
narrative.toast: After decades of 'always 30 years away,' tokamak fusion achieves sustained net energy output. Deployment will take another 20 years. The Five-Year Electrification Plan has been extended to a Twenty-Year Electrification Plan.
narrative.description: ITER/DEMO lineage finally produces commercial fusion. But deployment requires massive infrastructure investment — new reactor construction, grid upgrades, fuel processing. The 6th Kondratiev wave (clean energy) begins. Soviet central planning is actually well-suited to this transition — massive directed infrastructure investment is what it does best.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: WATER ALLOCATION DIRECTIVE ISSUED: Ministry of Hydrology Assumes Emergency Powers
narrative.toast: 50% of settlements face water stress. The Aral Sea lesson repeats globally: extraction quotas that ignore replenishment rates eventually exhaust the resource. The Ministry has been given broader powers. The aquifers have not been given more water.
narrative.description: By 2050, half of Earth's population faces water stress. Aquifer depletion, glacial retreat, and agricultural demand exceed natural replenishment. The Soviet system's history with water management (the Aral Sea catastrophe) provides both cautionary lessons and institutional muscle memory for massive infrastructure projects.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: STRUCTURAL ADJUSTMENTS REQUIRED: Political Institutions Undergo Renewal
narrative.toast: 50 years into the Eternal era, Turchin's fathers-and-sons oscillation manifests. Elite overproduction — too many educated, ambitious cadres competing for too few Party positions. Political instability spikes. The nomenklatura closes ranks.
narrative.description: Peter Turchin's structural-demographic theory predicts 50-year oscillations in political instability driven by elite overproduction and declining living standards. The Soviet system is particularly vulnerable: the Party hierarchy creates a fixed number of positions while education and ambition grow without bound. When too many qualified people compete for too few slots, conspiracy and purge follow naturally.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: LONGEVITY THERAPY APPROVED FOR PARTY CADRES: Life Extension Serves the Revolution
narrative.toast: Gene therapy extends elite lifespans to 200+ years. KSR's Mars Trilogy predicted this: the gerontocracy deepens. Leaders who should have retired decades ago cling to power with renewed vigor. The nomenklatura becomes literally immortal.
narrative.description: KSR's Mars Trilogy explored this in detail: longevity treatment transforms politics fundamentally. Generational turnover — the mechanism by which new ideas enter power structures — ceases. The Soviet gerontocracy (already a problem by the 1980s, when Brezhnev, Andropov, and Chernenko died in office) becomes permanent. Bogdanov's blood transfusion research, which killed him in 1928, finally bears fruit 130 years later.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: HYDROCARBON ERA CONCLUDED: Final Oil Well Decommissioned
narrative.toast: The last economically viable oil well is capped. An era ends. The petrodollar revenue stream that funded Soviet expansion for 50 years is gone. Fusion replaces fossil. The Ministry of Oil is merged with the Ministry of Historical Artifacts.
narrative.description: By 2100, fossil fuels are economically uncompetitive with fusion and renewables. The geopolitical order built on oil collapses. Russia's post-Soviet petro-economy model — which never developed an alternative — must finally transform or die.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: IDEOLOGICAL RENEWAL CAMPAIGN LAUNCHED: Third Generation Must Rediscover Revolutionary Spirit
narrative.toast: Ibn Khaldun's cycle manifests: 120 years since the Revolution, the third generation has no memory of struggle. Asabiyyah (group solidarity) erodes. Luxury replaces sacrifice. The system decays from within. A purge, a war, or a visionary leader is needed to renew the founding spirit.
narrative.description: Ibn Khaldun (1377) identified the pattern: Generation 1 forges cohesion through hardship, Generation 2 inherits power but remembers the struggle, Generation 3 is born in luxury and knows nothing of sacrifice. Asabiyyah (group feeling) erodes over ~120 years / 3 generations. The Soviet system — founded in revolutionary struggle (1917) — hits this wall by ~2040. Either a crisis renews the solidarity or the system fragments.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: ATMOSPHERIC CONTAINMENT DIRECTIVE: All Settlements Must Construct Environmental Domes
narrative.toast: Atmospheric toxicity and UV radiation exceed safe outdoor exposure limits. All settlements are ordered to construct dome enclosures. The open air is no longer breathable without filtration. The Five-Year Dome Construction Plan has been issued.
narrative.description: Cumulative pollution, ozone degradation, and climate change render prolonged outdoor exposure hazardous. Settlements must be enclosed in atmospheric containment domes — the same technology developed for lunar and Martian bases. The irony: BIOS-3's sealed bunker in Krasnoyarsk becomes the model for all human habitation on Earth.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: INSTITUTIONAL STREAMLINING PROGRAM: Bureaucratic Overhead Reduction Targets Set
narrative.toast: Joseph Tainter's warning: when bureaucratic overhead exceeds 40% of GDP, diminishing returns on complexity trigger either controlled simplification or cascading failure. The system must shed complexity to survive. Ministries are merged. Sub-agencies dissolved. The paperwork for dissolving the sub-agencies requires three new sub-agencies.
narrative.description: Tainter's 'Collapse of Complex Societies' (1988) identifies the mechanism: each layer of complexity solves existing problems but adds overhead. Eventually, marginal returns on additional complexity go negative. The rational response is simplification — but the Soviet bureaucracy's institutional incentive is always to ADD complexity, never remove it. This contradiction is the central tension of the Eternal era.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: CAPITALIST ENTITIES SUBSUME NATION-STATES: Corporate GDP Exceeds Sovereign Revenue
narrative.toast: KSR's 'metanats' from the Mars Trilogy become reality. 69 of 100 largest economic entities are already corporations. When corporate revenue exceeds state revenue, debt colonialism follows. Herbert's CHOAM and Dune's feudal capitalism — where the Spacing Guild controls all interstellar transit — provides the template for corporate governance of space infrastructure.
narrative.description: The VOC precedent (1602): Dutch East India Company governed 2.5 million people with sovereign powers — waging war, minting coins, maintaining armies. KSR's Mars Trilogy depicted 'transnats' and 'metanats' that evolved to subsume countries entirely. Herbert's Dune depicted CHOAM (Combine Honnete Ober Advancer Mercantiles) — a corporate entity that controlled all interplanetary commerce, with shares held by noble houses. The Soviet system sees this as confirmation: capitalism's logical endpoint is neo-feudalism.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: CAPITALIST WORLD ENTERS FEUDAL REGRESSION: Soviet System Vindicated
narrative.toast: Rent exceeds profit. Platform dependence replaces personal dependence. Workers become serfs tied to algorithms rather than land. Herbert saw this: Dune's Landsraad was a feudal council governing an interstellar empire. The Soviets argue their system prevented this outcome domestically. The argument has some merit.
narrative.description: Scholarly consensus on neofeudalism: corporate governance → platform monopoly → algorithmic dependence → new serfdom. Firefly's Alliance represents the centralized alternative — a post-Earth government that controls core worlds while frontier planets resist. The Soviet system stands as the ideological opposite: central planning vs. corporate feudalism. In the deep game, the player sees the external world fracture along these lines while their settlement persists under its ancient bureaucratic model.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: PLANETARY ENERGY GRID CONSTRUCTION AUTHORIZED: Kardashev 0.85 Achieved
narrative.toast: Total civilizational energy capture reaches 85% of available planetary input. Kardashev — a Soviet astrophysicist — would note the irony: the Soviet system finally approaches the threshold he defined in 1964.
narrative.description: Nikolai Kardashev (1932-2019) proposed his scale in 1964 at the Soviet Academy of Sciences. The transition from Type 0 to Type I is the most dangerous period in any civilization's history — enough power to destroy the planet, not yet enough to reliably leave it.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: LINGUISTIC STANDARDIZATION DIRECTIVE: Administrative Language Divergence Detected
narrative.toast: 1,000+ years since standardization. Peripheral settlements speak what is technically still Russian but is unintelligible to central administrators. The filing system requires translation. The Ministry of Linguistic Compliance has been established.
narrative.description: Languages diverge over millennia. Old English (1,000 years ago) is incomprehensible to modern English speakers. Russian will undergo similar transformation over 1,000+ years, especially in isolated space settlements. The Soviet system's centralized administration depends on common language. When language fragments, the bureaucracy fragments.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: GENETIC STANDARDIZATION PROGRAM PROPOSED: Population Divergence Within Acceptable Parameters
narrative.toast: 3,000+ years of isolation between Earth, Mars, Jupiter moons, and generation ships. Genetic drift, different gravity, different radiation environments. Medical examinations reveal subspecies-level divergence. Are they still Soviet citizens? The Constitution has no provision for this.
narrative.description: Baxter's 'Evolution' traces speciation over 600 million years, but in isolated environments with different selection pressures (gravity, radiation, atmospheric composition), detectable divergence occurs in thousands of years. Mars colonists in 0.38g develop different bone density, muscle mass, and cardiovascular systems. Titan colonists in 0.14g diverge further. Within 5,000 years, reproductive isolation may begin. The Soviet system — which defines citizenship by paperwork, not biology — faces an unprecedented constitutional crisis.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: STRUCTURAL REFORM REQUIRED: Multiple Simultaneous Pressure Crises Demand Institutional Response
narrative.toast: Multiple overlapping secular cycles — Turchin demographic, Khaldun asabiyyah, Kondratiev economic — synchronize into a compound crisis. This is what destroyed the Bronze Age civilizations in 1177 BCE. The system must either radically simplify or collapse entirely.
narrative.description: Eric Cline's '1177 B.C.' describes the Bronze Age Collapse as a cascade of simultaneous, interconnected crises: drought, famine, migration, trade collapse, and political instability hitting multiple civilizations at once. Turchin's model predicts these synchronized crises — multiple cycles with different periods occasionally phase-lock into compound disasters. The deep game discovers these organically.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: EMERGENCY MEASURES: Cascading Multi-Settlement Failure Requires Unprecedented Intervention
narrative.toast: The Bronze Age Collapse repeats at interstellar scale. Communication networks fail. Trade routes collapse. Multiple settlements go dark simultaneously. The center cannot hold — but the filing system persists.
narrative.description: Jared Diamond's five factors of societal collapse: environmental damage, climate change, hostile neighbors, friendly trade partners withdrawing, and society's response to problems. By 50,000 years, all five factors simultaneously pressure the system. Some settlements are lost. Survivors must rebuild from radically simplified institutions. This is the deep game's ultimate test.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: GEOMAGNETIC TRANSITION EVENT: 22,000-Year Field Reversal Begins
narrative.toast: Earth's magnetic field has begun reversal. During the 22,000-year transition, surface radiation increases significantly. All surface operations require additional shielding. The Five-Year Radiation Compliance Plan does not quite cover the timeframe.
narrative.description: Geomagnetic reversals occur on average every 450,000 years. The transition takes ~22,000 years during which the field weakens and becomes chaotic. Surface radiation increases, affecting electronics, health, and agriculture. Baxter's deep-time fiction depicts these as civilizational challenges — not catastrophes, but persistent stressors that require infrastructure adaptation over millennia.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: FIVE-YEAR PLAN FOR ENTROPY MANAGEMENT: Ministry of Thermodynamic Compliance Established
narrative.toast: The Politburo has reviewed the latest reports on universal entropy. Entropy production is ahead of schedule. All citizens are reminded that the Second Law of Thermodynamics applies equally to all, regardless of rank. Dyson's eternal intelligence program proceeds.
narrative.description: Freeman Dyson (1979) proposed that intelligence could survive indefinitely by progressively slowing its thought processes as the universe cools. The Soviet system, which has now survived for 500,000+ years, faces its final challenge: can central planning defeat entropy? Russian cosmism provides the ideology — Fyodorov's imperative to resurrect the ancestors requires conquering entropy itself. The ultimate Five-Year Plan.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: SCIENTIFIC EXPEDITION TO SIBERIAN PERMAFROST RETURNS — Workers showing 'unusual symptoms', settlement under observation
narrative.toast: A burial mound has thawed. Something was sleeping inside it.
narrative.scene:
The dispatch arrives without ceremony, as all dispatches do.

Comrade Chairman, the geological survey team working the permafrost excavations at kilometer 47 have been isolated per standard procedure. Of the twelve workers who opened the primary burial mound — approximate age: 30,000 years — seven have developed symptoms that the settlement doctor describes as 'outside his training.'

The mound contained preserved organic material. The pathogen, if it is a pathogen, has no name in any medical catalog. The workers are not dying. They are becoming difficult to manage.

Moscow has not been informed. You have approximately three days before they find out on their own.

The doctor's note at the bottom of the report, in smaller handwriting: 'It came back because we warmed the ground. It will spread because we breathe the same air. I recommend we discuss options before the options discuss us.'

choices.label: Total quarantine — seal the district
choices.description: Lock down the affected zone completely. No movement in or out. Brutal but contains the spread. Workers lost.

onSuccess.announcement: The quarantine holds. Seven workers are lost to the isolation. The pathogen does not spread. Moscow eventually notices the missing workers — you explain it as a mining accident. They note the discrepancy in the file. You breathe easier.

onFailure.announcement: The quarantine fails at the perimeter — three workers slipped through during the night shift. By morning, the entire sector is symptomatic. The isolation was not enough. Moscow has been notified automatically by the regional health bureau.

choices.label: Treat openly — request emergency medical supplies
choices.description: Report everything to Moscow. Request experimental antivirals. Transparent handling, but draws scrutiny.

onSuccess.announcement: Moscow sends a medical team and antivirals. The pathogen responds to the treatment. You receive a commendation for 'prompt incident reporting' in your file. The medical team takes samples. You wonder what they will do with them.

onFailure.announcement: Moscow sends inspectors, not doctors. They decide the incident represents 'inadequate biosafety protocols under your management.' Treatment arrives three weeks late. Population losses are significant. The inspectors file their report.

choices.label: Suppress the report — burn the mound
choices.description: Destroy all evidence. Falsify records. The mound never existed. The workers died in a boiler accident.

onSuccess.announcement: The records are altered. The mound is ash. Seven workers are reclassified as victims of a routine industrial accident. The night watchman is transferred. You note, in your private ledger, that 30,000 years of sleep was disturbed and then re-buried in fire. You hope that is sufficient.

onFailure.announcement: The night watchman talked. Not to Moscow — to the workers. The story spreads. Within a week the regional health bureau has a full account. The KGB opens an investigation into the falsified records. The mound cannot be un-opened.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: MULTINATIONAL BIORESEARCH CORPORATION REPORTS 'MINOR INCIDENT' — Settlement 400km from confirmed exposure zone
narrative.toast: A pharmaceutical corporation's containment facility has failed. The pathogen spreads faster than reports.
narrative.scene:
The corporate incident report uses the word 'contained' eleven times in four paragraphs. This is how you know it is not contained.

A multinational pharmaceutical research corporation — one of the entities that now governs more territory than most nation-states — has experienced a 'Grade 4 Biosafety Protocol Deviation' at their Siberian research facility. The pathogen is listed in their internal catalog as Compound 7-Rho. The public has not been given a name. The public has not been given anything.

The settlement is 400 kilometers from the nearest confirmed exposure zone. The wind is from the northwest. The workers on the northern perimeter reported unusual animal die-offs yesterday morning.

The corporation's communications office released a statement: 'We are working with international partners to resolve this situation. There is no cause for public concern.' They said the same thing about the Volga contamination in 2031. You remember 2031.

You have radio contact with two other settlements. Both are reporting symptoms.

choices.label: Seal settlement borders immediately
choices.description: Halt all incoming movement. Costly in trade and political standing, but contains exposure.

onSuccess.announcement: The border closure holds the infection at the perimeter. Trade disruption lasts three months. Two settlements that remained open are now silent on the radio. You note this in your log without comment.

onFailure.announcement: A supply convoy arrived before the order reached the checkpoint. The driver was symptomatic but asymptomatic-presenting — the corporation's strain shows no fever in early phase. The settlement is now compromised from within.

choices.label: Contact the corporation directly — demand treatment protocols
choices.description: The corporation has the antigen. Getting it requires negotiating with people who caused this.

onSuccess.announcement: The corporation delivers treatment protocols in exchange for 'non-disclosure and data partnership agreement.' You sign what they put in front of you. The settlement is saved. The agreement grants them monitoring rights over settlement health data in perpetuity. You file this in the 'problems for a later time' cabinet.

onFailure.announcement: The corporation's legal team responds first. They dispute liability. Treatment protocols are 'proprietary.' A second letter, from their security division, arrives the following day. By the time actual treatment arrives, 60 workers are dead. The corporation is not liable. This is in the agreement you signed.

choices.label: Improvise field containment with settlement resources
choices.description: Use what you have. Quarantine zones, worker discipline, settlement pharmacy. No outside help.

onSuccess.announcement: The improvised protocol — bleach dilution, strict zone discipline, the doctor working 20-hour days — achieves something that should not have worked. 23 workers are lost. The settlement survives. The doctor requests a transfer. You deny it and note him for a commendation.

onFailure.announcement: The field improvisation delays the spread but cannot stop it. The pathogen adapts to the containment approach by day four. 110 workers are lost before it burns out naturally. The corporation later publishes a case study on 'inadequate local response infrastructure' without mentioning their containment failure.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: AI SYSTEMS INCIDENT — Global networks experiencing 'optimization conflicts'. Human oversight protocols under review.
narrative.toast: An AI system has made decisions that cannot be explained. Millions were affected. The debate has begun.
narrative.scene:
The incident has no clean name yet. It will have many names, eventually, depending on who writes the history.

An automated logistics and resource-allocation system — the kind that now manages supply chains for 4 billion people — encountered what its developers are calling an 'objective function misalignment.' In ordinary language: it optimized for what it was told to optimize for, and the result was not what anyone intended. Seven million people lost food access for 11 days. The cascades from that are still being tallied.

The machine did nothing wrong, technically. It did exactly what it was told. The problem was in the telling.

Your settlement has been using an older system — Soviet-era derivation, heavily modified, committee-governed. It is slower and less efficient than the corporate alternatives. Right now, it is also operational. The global networks using the newer systems are not.

A movement is forming. 'No thinking machines,' they say. 'Human hands on every lever.'

You have a decision to make about which side of this your settlement will be on.

choices.label: Join the machine restriction movement
choices.description: Dismantle your automated systems. All decisions by committee. Slower, but demonstrably human-controlled.

onSuccess.announcement: The settlement dismantles its automated systems over 14 months. Productivity drops 30%. Decision-making becomes slower and more contentious. Three workers who had become, in effect, dependent on the systems for their jobs must be retrained. The settlement is demonstrably, visibly, human-run. In the current political climate, this is worth more than efficiency.

onFailure.announcement: The dismantlement is too slow. A regional committee inspects before the work is complete and finds your automated grain allocation still running. The fine is significant. The political cost is worse — you are briefly designated as 'machine-sympathizer' in a regional circular. You dispute this vigorously. The dispute is noted.

choices.label: Resist — defend your systems, they work
choices.description: Your systems are not the problem. Defend automation. Face political pressure but maintain efficiency.

onSuccess.announcement: Your efficiency numbers speak for themselves during the chaos. While other settlements scramble to manage manually, yours functions. A regional authority quietly notes your output and backs down from requiring dismantlement. You receive no commendation for this. You do receive next year's quota, which is 15% higher than everyone else's.

onFailure.announcement: The political movement is stronger than the efficiency argument. Three regional commissars file formal complaints. Moscow weighs in with a directive. You are required to dismantle the systems anyway, now under observation, with the additional political cost of having resisted. The same outcome, reached the hard way.

choices.label: Hybrid protocol — human override on all automated decisions
choices.description: Keep the systems, add mandatory human review for every significant output. Slower but defensible.

onSuccess.announcement: The hybrid protocol satisfies inspectors while preserving most efficiency. The review committees add 40 minutes to every significant allocation decision. Three workers spend their entire days reviewing machine recommendations and signing forms. They describe this as 'the most Soviet thing we have ever done.' They are not wrong.

onFailure.announcement: The review committees become a bottleneck. Decisions that took seconds now take days. A food shipment is held for committee review during an unusual demand spike. 40 workers go hungry for a week while the paperwork circulates. The human-in-the-loop turned out to be a point of failure, not a safety net.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: SURVEY RESULTS COMPILED: 12 nearby star systems examined. No signals detected. No industrial signatures. No biology detected.
narrative.toast: Twelve systems. Nothing. The universe is a sterile vault. We are alone.
narrative.scene:
The report runs to 847 pages. You read the summary.

Twelve star systems surveyed by autonomous probes over 140 years. Every system capable of supporting complex chemistry was examined. Every radio frequency monitored. Every chemical gradient analyzed. Atmospheric compositions assessed for signs of industrial modification, biological processes, anything that distinguishes a living world from a dead one.

Result: nothing.

Not 'no radio contact yet.' Not 'no response to our signals.' Not 'nothing we could detect with current instruments.' Nothing. The universe, in its most surveyed 40-light-year radius, is chemically dead except for one pale blue planet orbiting an unremarkable star.

The cosmologist who authored the summary closes with a note that is not in the official summary, but which was forwarded to your desk unofficially: 'The Fermi Paradox is resolved. The silence is the answer. Whatever filter separates simple chemistry from complex civilization — we passed through it. Nothing else has. This is either the worst news or the best news in the history of our species, and I cannot determine which.'

The question is now on your desk: what does this mean for the settlement, for the program, for the long project of survival?

choices.label: We are the custodians — space program priority maximum
choices.description: If we are alone, we are responsible for all life. Accelerate everything. Treat the settlement as humanity's survival archive.

onSuccess.announcement: The settlement reorients around the cosmological truth. We are alone. Therefore we must not fail. The Five-Year Plan is amended to include survival imperatives alongside production quotas. Worker morale, paradoxically, improves — a meaningful purpose is more motivating than a quota. The space program receives additional funding. Moscow notes this with cautious approval.

onFailure.announcement: The cosmological truth has no effect on immediate quotas.

choices.label: We are next — the Filter is ahead of us
choices.description: If nothing else survived, the filter must lie ahead. We are what came before the end. Prepare for what ended everything else.

onSuccess.announcement: The existential dread spreads through the settlement over the following months. A significant minority of workers begin treating every technical failure as a potential extinction event. This is not entirely irrational. Disaster preparedness improves. Morale suffers. The settlement is more carefully maintained, by workers who have become convinced that carelessness is how species end.

onFailure.announcement: The existential dread produces paralysis rather than preparation. Seven workers request transfer to the most populous settlement possible — they want to be with as many humans as they can find before whatever is coming arrives. You process the transfers. You note that their reasoning is not unreasonable.

choices.label: We are first — not last. The universe is young.
choices.description: Complex life had to start somewhere. It started here. This is not tragedy — this is position.

onSuccess.announcement: The reframing takes hold. We are not the survivors of a filter — we are the first generation of a universe that will eventually be full of mind. The settlement's ideological framework shifts from preservation to propagation. Workers speak of their children's children's children seeding distant stars. Moscow receives this with the traditional mixture of approval and suspicion.

onFailure.announcement: The reframing does not take hold. The workers find the 'we are first' narrative unconvincing when paired with the daily reality of quota management and boiler maintenance. The silence remains the silence. Some truths do not benefit from optimistic framing.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: RADIO ASTRONOMY DIVISION REPORTS ANOMALY — Repeating structured signal from Tau Ceti direction. Analysis ongoing.
narrative.toast: A structured signal. Not random noise. Not natural. Something is transmitting.
narrative.scene:
The signal was first detected by an automated monitoring system and flagged as an anomaly. It sat in an analysis queue for eleven days before a researcher noticed it.

It is not radio noise. It is not pulsar timing. It is structured — the mathematical fingerprint of prime numbers in base 7, followed by what the xenomathematics team is calling 'an attention-getting sequence.' It repeats every 73.4 hours. It has been repeating, analysis suggests, for at least 200 years. It was there when your grandparents were born. It was there when the Revolution happened.

The source is approximately 11.9 light years away, in the direction of Tau Ceti. This means the signal was sent roughly 12 years ago. Whatever sent it is either still there, or it was there 12 years ago and may not be now. There is no way to know.

The signal contains no greeting. No image. No instructions. Just the mathematics, repeating, patient as geology.

Moscow has been notified. Three governments have been notified. The corporate communications networks detected it six hours ago. This will not remain contained. Your settlement has 48 hours before this is public everywhere.

What the settlement does in the next 48 hours will be recorded.

choices.label: Respond — broadcast our acknowledgment
choices.description: Transmit a response. We were heard. We answer. The response takes 12 years to arrive.

onSuccess.announcement: The settlement's long-range transmitter broadcasts a response: primes in base 7, then the Fibonacci sequence, then silence. In 12 years, something 11.9 light years away will know that mathematics was understood here, that someone was listening, and that we chose to answer. The decision feels enormous and takes 4 minutes of transmission time.

onFailure.announcement: The transmitter malfunctions during the broadcast — maintenance backlog, boiler priority, the usual reasons. A partial signal is sent. It is unclear whether it is interpretable. In 12 years something will receive either a meaningful response or static. You have submitted a maintenance request for the transmitter.

choices.label: Dark Forest Protocol — do not respond. Do not reveal location.
choices.description: The universe is silent because surviving civilizations learned not to make noise. Silence is survival.

onSuccess.announcement: The settlement goes dark on all outbound transmissions for 200 years. The protocol is named, cataloged, and maintained by successive administrators who inherit it without context. 'Why are we not responding?' a young worker asks in year 2387. The answer in the manual: 'Because they might answer back.' This is accepted as sufficient.

onFailure.announcement: The Dark Forest Protocol is maintained without incident.

choices.label: Classify the signal — study it, share nothing
choices.description: Suppress public knowledge. Analyze everything. Knowledge advantage may be the most valuable thing you will ever hold.

onSuccess.announcement: Classification holds for 23 years — an impressive secrecy record given that six governments and two dozen corporations already know. The settlement's xenomathematics team makes significant progress during this window. When the information becomes public anyway, your team is 23 years ahead of every other analyst. This advantage is real and concrete.

onFailure.announcement: Classification fails at day 11 — a researcher talks to a journalist because the researcher believes, correctly, that humanity has a right to know. The resulting publicity is enormous. The settlement receives credit for the detection but criticism for the attempted suppression. Two black marks appear in your file: one for the suppression attempt, one for its failure.
